channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Mississippi health system shuts down clinics statewide after ransomware attack
item.description: The attack was launched on Thursday and prompted hospital officials to close all of its 35 clinics across the state.

item.title: Blizzard conditions and high winds forecast for NYC, East coast
item.description: The winter storm is expected to bring blizzard conditions and possibly up to 2 feet of snow in New York City.

item.title: Vietnam Veterans sue to block proposed 'Independence Arch' near Arlington National Cemetery
item.description: The lawsuit challenges President Trump's plans for "Independence Arch," a 250-foot structure proposed for Memorial Circle.

item.title: Norway's Johannes Klæbo is new Winter Olympics king
item.description: Johannes Klaebo won all six cross-country skiing events at this year's Winter Olympics, the surpassing Eric Heiden's five golds in 1980.

item.title: Trump to raise global tariffs to 15%
item.description: President Trump previously said he would implement 10% global tariffs after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down his tariff policies.

item.title: Giant tortoises return to Galápagos island after nearly 200 years
item.description: The native species was driven to extinction by sailors in the 1800s. Now, 158 juvenile giant tortoises have been reintroduced to the island.

item.title: Blizzard warnings issued for New York City, New Jersey, Connecticut as storm threatens East Coast
Storms threaten to pummel East Coast as blizzard warnings issued
item.description: Blizzard warnings were issued Saturday for New York City, New Jersey and coastal communities along the East Coast.

item.title: Killing of nationalist student leaves French far left in deep trouble as elections loom
item.description: Far-left militants are suspected of being behind Quentin Deranque's death and the party of Jean-Luc Mélenchon is being widely condemned.

item.title: Iran students stage first anti-government protests since deadly crackdown
item.description: The student protesters honoured thousands of those killed when nationwide mass protests were put down last month.

item.title: Which countries and athletes have the most Olympic medals of all time?
item.description: While the U.S. is on top of the overall Olympic medal count, it is not the country that dominates winter sports like the 2026 Milano Cortina Games in ...

item.title: Nasa astronauts' moon mission delayed due to rocket issue
item.description: The mission to the far side of the Moon and back will be postponed after problems with were spotted with its rocket, a Nasa official said.

item.title: Former "American Idol" contestant charged in wife's murder in Ohio
item.description: Caleb Flynn, 37, appeared in season 12 of "American Idol." He was arrested by Tipp City police last week and charged with murder, assault and tamperin...

item.title: Team USA captures record-breaking 11th gold medal at Winter Games
item.description: Team USA could add more gold medals to its tally, including on Sunday when the U.S. men's hockey team takes on Team Canada.

item.title: Jordan Stolz is 4th in the last Olympic speedskating race after winning gold
item.description: U.S. speedskater Jordan Stolz finished fourth in his last race after winning two golds and a silver.

item.title: Giant tortoises return to Galápagos island after nearly 200 years
item.description: One hundred and fifty eight captive-bred juvenile tortoises were released on the Galápagos island of Floreana.

item.title: NASA moon rocket hit by new problem expected to bump astronauts’ lunar trip into April
item.description: NASA’s new moon rocket has suffered another setback, almost certain to bump astronauts’ lunar trip into spring.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Trump’s sweeping tariffs, sparking fierce pushback and vow of new levies
